question_text: A household uses 12,000 kWh of electricity per year. The energy mix comes from: 40% natural gas (0.2 kg CO₂ per kWh), 35% nuclear (0.01 kg CO₂ per kWh), and 25% wind (0.01 kg CO₂ per kWh). Calculate the total carbon footprint in kilograms of CO₂ produced annually.
explanation: Calculate CO₂ from each source separately: natural gas (40% × 12,000 × 0.2 = 960 kg), nuclear (35% × 12,000 × 0.01 = 42 kg), wind (25% × 12,000 × 0.01 = 30 kg). Adding these gives 1032 kg total. Note: correct answer is 1032 kg CO₂; if calculation shows 960 + 42 + 30 = 1032, this is the target answer.
